title: Odyssey, Book 8: Songs and Games
description: Book 8 of Homer's Odyssey: Songs and Games.
introduction: Book 8 of the Odyssey: Songs and Games. The Greek text follows the standard Homeric vulgate.

translation.text: Alcinous held games in Odysseus' honor. The bard Demodocus sang of the quarrel between Odysseus and Achilles, and Odysseus wept, hiding his tears in his cloak. He joined the games and threw the discus farther than any Phaeacian. Demodocus then sang of Ares and Aphrodite caught in Hephaestus' net, and finally of the Trojan Horse. Again Odysseus wept. Alcinous asked: 'Who are you?'
